name: Toasted Almond Soup the Inn at Long Last
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a large soup pot with Pam cooking spray. Add the stock and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low, then add the rice and blanched almonds. Cover and cook slowly for 30 minutes.
2. In a separate 10-inch skillet, spray with Pam cooking spray and melt the butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until it starts to soften, about 3 1/2 minutes. Lower the heat slightly, then add the finely chopped garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for about 20 seconds.
Stir in the sherry, then crumble in the almond paste a bit at a time. Remove the skillet from the heat and whisk until smooth.
3. Reduce the heat under the stock to the barest possible simmer. Add the onion mixture, orange zest, and evaporated skim milk. Let the soup blend flavors for 10 minutes more.
4. Puree the soup in batches using a food processor. Pass it through a food mill or fine strainer. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
5. Reheat just before serving and serve hot in small cups. Garnish each serving with toasted almonds and a sprinkle of orange zest.
